title: Speedboat Hire Hourly in Marbella: Costs and Tips for 2026
html:
Marbella offers a stunning coastline perfect for hourly speedboat hire, with prices starting from around €200 per hour. Depart from Puerto Banús or Marbella Marina, and use BoatHire24 to compare verified boat listings for your ideal trip.

Where to set off in Marbella

Puerto Banús is the most famous departure point, with its glamorous atmosphere and direct access to the Mediterranean. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo de Marbella) offers a more relaxed base near the old town. Cabopino Marina, east of Marbella, is quieter and closer to the dunes. For a day trip, head towards the coastline of Estepona or Málaga, or anchor off the island of Isla Verde. The bay of Marbella provides calm waters ideal for speedboat exploration.

Which boat to rent

  1. Speedboat (e.g. Axopar 28), best for a thrilling hour of water sports or coastal cruising, up to 8 guests, from €250 per hour.
  2. Motor yacht (e.g. Azimut 58), ideal for luxury half-day charter with spacious deck, 10 guests, from €800 per hour. Check this motor yacht for a detailed example.
  3. RIBs/Open boats, excellent for a fast ride to more distant coves, 6 guests, from €180 per hour.
  4. Small runabout, perfect for couples or small groups wanting a self-drive experience, 4 guests, from €120 per hour.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Marbella?

Hourly speedboat hire in Marbella ranges from €120 to €1,500 per hour depending on boat size, season and crew. For a shared tour, expect to pay from €60 per person. A private small motorboat for a half-day (4 hours) costs around €480 to €800. Catamaran full-day hire for up to 12 guests starts at €1,500. A crewed luxury yacht (like a Sunseeker) can be €2,000 per hour. Peak months (June to September) command higher rates, and many operators require a minimum booking of two hours.

  • Shared tour: from €60 per person per hour
  • Small motorboat half-day: from €480 for 4 hours
  • Catamaran full-day: from €1,500 for 8 hours, up to 12 guests
  • Crewed luxury yacht: from €2,000 per hour

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Marbella?

For most speedboats up to 15hp, you do not need a licence, though many operators rent more powerful boats with a qualified skipper. In Spain, the law requires a licence for boats over 15hp or longer than 6 metres. However, virtually all hourly hires in Marbella include a skipper. Always confirm with the operator; BoatHire24 lists each boat’s licence requirements clearly.

Tip: Book morning slots (9-11am) in July and August to avoid the levante wind which picks up in the afternoon and can make the sea choppy.

At a glance

  • Price range: €120, €1,500 per hour
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September
  • Common hire duration: 2, 4 hours
  • Max guests without extra cost: 8, 12
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can I hire a speedboat without a skipper in Marbella?

Most hourly charters include a skipper; bareboat rental (without skipper) is rare and requires a valid Spanish boating licence or an ICC.

What is the best time of year for speedboat hire in Marbella?

The best weather runs from May to October, with July and August being the hottest and busiest months; September still offers warm seas and lighter crowds.

How far can I go in a one-hour speedboat hire?

A typical one-hour trip from Puerto Banús covers the bay to Cabopino Marina, about 8 nautical miles each way, with a short stop for swimming.

What should I bring for a speedboat hire in Marbella?

Bring sunscreen, a hat, a towel, and a light jacket for wind, plus snacks and drinks if the boat has a cooler; most charters provide safety gear and ice.

Whether you are after a quick thrill or a sunset champagne cruise, hourly speedboat hire in Marbella is easy to arrange. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own. If you own a boat, you can list a boat for free and start earning.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
